---
title: "Routing and Modular Intelligence"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "octopus_router"
  - "rmi"
  - "moecot"
  - "beastbrain"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "moecot_md"
  - "rgs"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Routing and Modular Intelligence

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `moecot`, `beastbrain`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `benchmaxxing`, `moecot_md`, `rgs` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from the handoff outline and source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The stack needs to assign work across bounded specialists while preserving permissions, readiness, and regression history.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

One undifferentiated system cannot cleanly express specialist capability, local memory, readiness gates, quarantine, or residual escrow.

## Core claim

[10-core, support: argument] ASI scales by routing across bounded specialist modules with explicit readiness gates and benchmark-driven promotion.

## Mechanism

- Use lightweight routing heads to select specialist cores.
- Track residuals, benchmark frontiers, and regression suites.
- Split, merge, retire, or quarantine modules based on evidence.

## Interfaces

- Planning asks for capability.
- Governance bounds route authority.
- Evidence layers decide readiness and promotion.

## Invariants

- Specialist authority is local and explicit.
- Promotion requires benchmark and regression evidence.
- Residuals are recorded instead of hidden.

## Failure modes

- Wrong specialist selection.
- Premature promotion.
- Regression loss after module replacement.

## Minimal implementation

A router registry with capability metadata, readiness gates, residual escrow, and regression preservation hooks.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Specialist routing accuracy test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Readiness gate enforcement test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Benchmark promotion test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Quarantine behavior test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Regression preservation test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Residual escrow integrity test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `octopus_router` | Octopus Router Architecture | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Lightweight head/router with dynamically loaded specialist arms and local boundaries. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |
| `beastbrain` | BeastBrain Cognitive Architecture | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Architecture lineage. Organism paradigm, SSD-native/geometrically verified intelligence. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `moecot_md` | moecot_agent_whitepaper.md | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Markdown export/source version. |
| `rgs` | Ratcheting Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Bridge between active compression, procedural memory, benchmark frontiers, verified AI growth. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest the assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
